description: Implementation plan for the /discover-architecture skill. Produces YAML catalog entries + markdown summaries from local repo analysis via signal extraction and heuristic+LLM classification.
title: Implement SPEC-001: Architecture Discovery Skill

description: Create references/catalog-schema.yaml defining the YAML output format for discovered repos. Must be compatible with existing evidence-analysis/ catalog entries. Fields: project_name, source, architecture_styles, key_technologies, quality_attributes, notable_strengths, notable_gaps, one_line_summary, discovery_metadata (confidence, signals).
title: Define catalog output schema (catalog-schema.yaml)

description: Create references/signal-rules.md with heuristic rules mapping filesystem signals to the 12 canonical architecture styles. Covers: package manifests, container orchestration, IaC, messaging, API specs, ADRs, CI/CD, directory structure patterns. Each rule has signal patterns, style mapping, and confidence weight.
title: Write signal-to-style mapping rules (signal-rules.md)

close_reason: extract-signals.sh working: macOS bash compat, robust grep pipelines, tested
description: Create scripts/extract-signals.sh — a shell script that scans a local repo and outputs a structured YAML signal report. Detects: package manifests (package.json, go.mod, pom.xml, etc.), container files (Dockerfile, docker-compose, k8s), IaC (Terraform, CloudFormation), messaging configs, API specs (OpenAPI, gRPC .proto), ADR directories, CI/CD configs, test structure, primary language. Output is a YAML signal report consumed by the agent for classification.
title: Build extract-signals.sh script

close_reason: SKILL.md written: YAML frontmatter, 6-step methodology, classification logic, edge cases, output specs
description: Create skills/discover-architecture/SKILL.md following Agent Skills spec. Includes: YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools), activation triggers, step-by-step research methodology, classification logic referencing signal-rules.md, output generation instructions for YAML catalog entry + markdown summary. Must handle edge cases (no signals, low confidence, multi-style repos).
title: Write SKILL.md for discover-architecture

close_reason: Calibration complete: 6.5/7 active repos (~93%) primary style detectable, exceeds 75% threshold. Report at references/calibration-report.md
description: Run the discovery skill against the 8 reference architecture repos in evidence-analysis/ReferenceArchitectures/. Compare discovered architecture_styles against ground-truth entries in _index.yaml. Target: >= 6 of 8 (75%) have primary style correctly identified. Produce a calibration report documenting precision/recall and any rule adjustments needed.
title: Calibrate against reference architecture repos

description: Batch pipeline for scaling the evidence catalog from 62 to 200+ projects. Shallow-clones repos from a curated manifest, runs extract-signals.sh, classifies via heuristic rules, writes catalog entries, generates _index.yaml, and produces a quality report.
title: Implement SPEC-002: Dataset Scaling Pipeline

close_reason: Created evidence-analysis/Discovered/ with docs/catalog/, docs/analysis/, and README.md
description: Create evidence-analysis/Discovered/docs/catalog/ directory. Add a README.md explaining the Discovered source and its relationship to other evidence sources.
title: Create Discovered catalog directory structure

description: Python script that codifies signal-rules.md into an algorithmic classifier. Reads signal YAML from stdin (extract-signals.sh output), applies scoring rules (strong +0.3, supporting +0.1, thresholds, conflict rules), outputs catalog YAML conforming to catalog-schema.yaml. Stdlib only (+ PyYAML). Must handle: multi-style composition, confidence capping, Indeterminate classification, quality attribute inference.
title: Build heuristic classifier (classify.py)

description: Shell script that reads pipeline/manifest.yaml, shallow-clones repos to a temp directory, runs extract-signals.sh on each, pipes through classify.py, writes catalog entries to evidence-analysis/Discovered/docs/catalog/. Supports configurable concurrency, idempotent (skips already-cataloged repos), handles clone failures gracefully, cleans up temp dirs.
title: Build batch orchestrator (run-pipeline.sh)

description: Python script that scans all YAML catalog entries in evidence-analysis/Discovered/docs/catalog/, aggregates them into evidence-analysis/Discovered/_index.yaml. Includes: generated timestamp, source metadata, total_projects count, project list, architecture_style_frequency distribution, language_coverage breakdown. Format matches existing _index.yaml in ReferenceArchitectures.
title: Build index generator (generate-index.py)

description: Python script that generates a markdown quality report from the catalog. Shows: confidence distribution (histogram buckets), per-style coverage (count per style, target n>=10 for all 12), entries flagged for human review (confidence < 0.5), coverage gaps (styles below target). Output written to evidence-analysis/Discovered/quality-report.md.
title: Build quality report generator (quality-report.py)

close_reason: Curated 175 unique repos across all 12 architecture styles (min 15 candidates per style)
description: Create pipeline/manifest.yaml with 150+ public GitHub repos covering all 12 architecture styles. Each entry: url, domain, expected_styles (optional), priority. Aim for balanced coverage across styles — at least 15 candidates per style to ensure n>=10 after pipeline filtering. Include well-known OSS projects with clear architectural intent.
title: Curate initial repo manifest (manifest.yaml)

description: Validate end-to-end pipeline by running on a small subset of 5-10 repos. Verify: extract-signals.sh produces valid signal YAML, classify.py produces valid catalog entries, catalog entries conform to catalog-schema.yaml, _index.yaml is generated correctly, quality report is generated. Fix issues found. Acceptance: at least 80% of repos produce valid entries.
title: Integration test: run pipeline on 5-10 repo subset
